label: Adapting Masterminds
summary: An Adapting Mastermind has only Mastermind Tactic cards (4 or 6) and no separate Mastermind card. The top Tactic counts as the current Mastermind and is kept face up. Whenever the Mastermind Adapts (during setup, after each Master Strike, and after each fight), shuffle the remaining Tactics and reveal a new one on top. You always fight the top Tactic.

label: Additional Mastermind
summary: When certain powerful Villains escape, they ascend to become additional Masterminds. These Masterminds have no Tactics and only need to be defeated once. All Masterminds must be defeated to win. When a Master Strike occurs, each Mastermind resolves its Strike in an order chosen by the current player.

label: Ambush Schemes
summary: Ambush Schemes are shuffled into the Villain Deck with their Villain Group. When played, place it next to the main Scheme and immediately resolve its Ambush effect. For the rest of the game, each Scheme Twist triggers both Schemes. Each Ambush Scheme lists how to defeat it for Victory Points. Only one Ambush Scheme can be in play at a time.

label: Asterisk Symbol (*)
summary: An asterisk (*) on a card’s Cost or Attack indicates that value is unusual or variable, such as Size‑Changing or a special condition required to fight. The asterisk itself has no rules effect beyond signaling that the value may change.

label: Choose a Villain Group
summary: When you are told to choose a Villain Group, you must choose one specific group by name. You may choose Henchman Groups, but you cannot choose multiple groups via shared words (e.g., choosing “Hydra” does not include Hydra Elite). Cards without a Villain Group never count.

label: Divided Card
summary: A Divided Card has two miniature cards printed on one card. You pay its cost once to recruit it. When played, choose one side and use only that side’s text. While not being played, it counts as having all printed colors, classes, names, and traits, and its printed values are the sum of both sides.

label: Enraging Wounds
summary: Enraging Wounds are shuffled into the Wound Deck with normal Wounds. You may play them from your hand for printed bonuses. Each has a unique Healing ability that can be used during your turn without preventing recruiting or fighting. They still count as Wounds for all effects.

label: Grievous Wounds
summary: Grievous Wounds are more difficult to heal than normal Wounds. Each has a special Healing ability such as spending Recruit to KO it. Using these Healing abilities does not prevent recruiting or fighting. Grievous Wounds still count as Wounds for all effects.

label: Locations
summary: Locations are played from the Villain Deck and placed above the nearest city space without a Location. They do not move and do not count as Villains. You may fight a Location by spending its listed Attack to claim it and resolve its Fight effect. If all spaces already have Locations, KO one with the lowest Attack.

label: Multiclass Cards
summary: Multiclass cards count as all of their listed Hero Classes simultaneously. They are especially effective for triggering Superpower abilities that require multiple classes and for abilities that count Hero Classes.

label: Multiple Masterminds
summary: Some effects create multiple Masterminds in the same game. Players must defeat all Masterminds to win. When a Master Strike occurs, each Mastermind resolves its Strike, in an order chosen by the current player.

label: Shards
summary: Shard tokens represent cosmic energy. Players may spend a Shard at any time during their turn for +1 Attack. Villains and Masterminds gain +1 Attack per Shard on them. When defeated, the player takes one Shard; the rest return to the supply.

label: Sidekicks
summary: Once per turn, a player may pay 2 Recruit to gain a Sidekick from the Sidekick Stack. When played, Sidekicks return to the bottom of the stack. They still count as Heroes when played and can trigger Superpower abilities.

label: Transforming Schemes
summary: Transforming Schemes are double‑sided. Start with the Setup side face up. When instructed to Transform, flip the Scheme and use only the rules on the new face.

label: Transforms
summary: When a card Transforms, remove it from the game and replace it with the specified transformed card. You complete all effects of the original card first, then may immediately play the transformed card if it enters your hand.

label: Traps
summary: When a Trap is played from the Villain Deck, it presents a challenge to complete this turn. If you succeed, put the Trap in your Victory Pile and score its VP. If you fail, suffer its penalty at end of turn. Traps never push Villains forward.

label: Unveiled Schemes
summary: An Unveiled Scheme is the transformed side of a Veiled Scheme. Once revealed, use only the Unveiled Scheme’s rules for the rest of the game unless another effect changes it.

label: Veiled Schemes
summary: Veiled Schemes begin face up. At a specified point, they are removed and replaced by a randomly selected Unveiled Scheme. The chosen Unveiled Scheme might be the reverse of the original card or a different one.

label: Villainous Weapons
summary: Villainous Weapons are not Villains. When played, they are captured by the Villain closest to the Villain Deck, granting its printed Attack bonus. If no Villain is present, the Weapon is KO’d. When you defeat a Villain or Mastermind holding Weapons, you gain them as Artifact cards with 0 cost.

label: Wounds on Villains
summary: Some effects place Wounds directly on Villains or Masterminds, giving them −1 Attack per Wound. When that enemy is defeated or leaves the city, all Wounds on it return to the Wound Stack. Wounds on enemies are ignored for their text while attached.
